title: Ice Cream, Breadcrumbs, Warm Dates & Olive Oil
summary: We want to talk about this breadcrumb topping… trust us, stale bread has never tasted better. You will never look back. The breadcrumbs are toasted in butter and brown sugar and will give you a summer’s worth of the best ice-cream topping. If there's one thing you should make of mine, it’s this!

ingredients.label: Drizzle Good-Quality Extra Virgin Olive Oil
ingredients.name: Extra Virgin Olive Oil

method.description: If you're making the breadcrumbs, remove and discard the crusts from the bread, then tear them into small chunks and blitz in a food processor to create fine-ish breadcrumbs.

Add the butter to a medium frying pan set over a medium heat. Allow it to melt and begin to foam, then add the breadcrumbs and stir, coating them in the butter. Stir often for 3-4 mins until the bread starts to turn light golden. Add the sugar and toss to coat again, then stir constantly for another 2 mins until they are golden brown. Tip onto a plate, then sprinkle with a little sea salt and allow to cool.
Pit the dates, transfer to a microwavable plate and microwave for 30 seconds on high. They might need a little longer, depending on your microwave.
Scoop balls of ice cream into bowls, then add a date or two and some cherries to each one, if using. Top with the breadcrumbs and a small drizzle of your best extra virgin olive oil.

chefs.name: Kitty Coles
chefs.summary: Kitty is a supremely talented cook and recipe writer. You can find her recipes here as well as in publications like the Irish Times, Gloss Magazine, and The Guardian. She divides her time between London, Dublin and Mallorca (where her parents live and dad grew up) and her food showcases seasonal ingredients with a splash of Mediterranean style. Kitty is a no-fuss, simple cook, who uses everyday ingredients to make delicious and easy recipes. You can find plenty more of Kitty's recipes in her cookbook: 'Make More With Less: Foolproof Recipes to Make Your Food Go Further'.
